mission.title: cert-manager: Support Azure Workload Identities
mission.description: Support Azure Workload Identities . Requested by 19+ users.

steps.title: Check current cert-manager deployment
steps.description:
Verify your cert-manager version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager
helm list -n cert-manager 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working cert-manager installation.

steps.title: Review cert-manager configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant cert-manager configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager
kubectl get configmap -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cert-manager
```
Azure recently added Azure Workload Identities (see https://github.com/Azure/azure-workload-identity and https://azure.github.io/azure-workload-identity/docs/) to AKS.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support Azure Workload Identities
steps.description:
### Pull Request Motivation

This PR adds the ability to to authenticate against Azure using Workload Identities. Closes #5085

Bumps `adal` version to 0.9.20 in order to make use of  `NewServicePrincipalTokenFromFederatedToken`

### Kind

feature

zure DNS
```

### Work in Progress!

I will need
```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: external-dns
  namespace: external-dns
  labels:
    azure.workload.identity/use: 'true'
  annotations:
    azure.workload.identity/client-id: bbbbbbbb-xxx-yyyyy-mmmm-rrrrrrrrrr
    azure.workload.identity/service-account-token-expiration: '86400'
    azure.workload.identity/tenant-id: yyyyyyy-xxx-zzzz-xxxx-rrrrrrrrrrrrr
```

steps.title: Upgrade cert-manager to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade cert-manager:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade cert-manager cert-manager/cert-manager --namespace cert-manager
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager
helm list -n cert-manager
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Support Azure Workload Identities " is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
### Pull Request Motivation

This PR adds the ability to to authenticate against Azure using Workload Identities. Closes #5085

Bumps `adal` version to 0.9.20 in order to make use of  `NewServicePrincipalTokenFromFederatedToken`

### Kind

feature

zure DNS
```

### Work in Progress!

I will need some help to get this across the line. In particular, I am fairly certain that the zz_generated..
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: external-dns
  namespace: external-dns
  labels:
    azure.workload.identity/use: 'true'
  annotations:
    azure.workload.identity/client-id: bbbbbbbb-xxx-yyyyy-mmmm-rrrrrrrrrr
    azure.workload.identity/service-account-token-expiration: '86400'
    azure.workload.identity/tenant-id: yyyyyyy-xxx-zzzz-xxxx-rrrrrrrrrrrrr

AZURE_AUTHORITY_HOST: https://login.microsoftonline.com/
AZURE_CLIENT_ID: bbbbbbbb-xxx-yyyyy-mmmm-rrrrrrrrrr
AZURE_FEDERATED_TOKEN_FILE: /var/run/secrets/azure/tokens/azure-identity-token
AZURE_TENANT_ID: yyyyyyy-xxx-zzzz-xxxx-rrrrrrrrrrrrr

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with cert-manager installed or the issue environment reproducible.
